memory.summary: I really want to join my friends for lunch instead of finishing the assignment, but I feel guilty about my plan. It's hard to say no without causing friction.

desires: Stay in the lunch with my friends so I don't have to explain why I'm late or cancel, even though I know I'll feel guilty later.
Get enough sleep tonight despite being tired now, because I really don't want to be exhausted during our plans tomorrow.
Finish this assignment quickly just so it's out of the way and I can focus entirely on hanging out with cohort_3.

goals.description: Join cohort_3 for lunch immediately to avoid the awkwardness of canceling or explaining a delay.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) outweighs my academic goals; avoiding conflict and maintaining closeness with friends is my top priority right now.

goals.description: Finish the assignment analysis just enough to mark it 'done' so I can fully focus on the meal without distraction.
goals.rationale: I have a short planning horizon and procrastination bias; doing this only as a quick hurdle clears my mind for socializing, even if it feels rushed.

agent.last_reasoning: I'll skip the assignment now. The lunch invite is too tempting to ignore, and canceling would feel awkward. My friends' happiness matters more than finishing this 'soft' task right now. I can just say I'm done or rush it later.
